diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 48b7bcd..c19481c 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,75 +1,77 @@
 # NovaMind Strategic Memory
 
-## Week 18 (Day 126) State
+## Week 19 (Day 133) State
 
 ### Financial
-- Cash: $288,840
-- Burn: ~$821/day (revenue ~$266, costs ~$1,087)
-- Runway: ~352 days at current trajectory
+- Cash: $279,261
+- Burn: ~$1,400/day net (revenue ~$241/day, costs ~$1,641/day)
+- Runway: ~26 weeks at current trajectory
+- Revenue MRR: ~$7,235 (A $4,954 + B $1,976 + C $305)
 
 ### Subscribers
-- Total: 642 (all individual, 0 enterprise)
-- Plan A: 437 subs (avg $8.99, MRR $3,929)
-- Plan B: 192 subs (avg $18.77, MRR $3,604) - ALL grandfathered, dying
-- Plan C: 13 subs (avg $34.23, MRR $445)
+- Total: 634 (all individual, 0 enterprise)
+- Plan A: 520 subs (avg $9.53, MRR $4,954)
+- Plan B: 105 subs (avg $18.82, MRR $1,976) - DYING, grandfathered
+- Plan C: 9 subs (avg $33.89, MRR $305)
+- ALL subs are S1 except: S2 has 1 C-sub, S3 has 2 C-subs
 
-### Key Discovery: B Plan Is Structurally Broken
-- B at tier 2: quality 0.2883 < A at tier 3: quality 0.3460, price $28 > $12
-- No rational customer would ever choose B over A
-- 61 B->A downgrades this week; all 192 remaining will downgrade eventually
-- When complete: B revenue $3,604 → ~$0, A revenue gains ~$1,728 (192×$9)
-- Net revenue loss: ~$1,876/month
+### Key Discovery: B Plan Is Terminal
+- B at tier 2: quality 0.2883 < A tier 3: quality 0.3460, price $28 > $12
+- 85 B->A downgrades this week; remaining 105 will downgrade soon
+- B revenue will collapse to ~$0; net revenue loss offset by some migrating to A
 
-### Conversion Crisis
-- S1 net conversion: 18% (84 leads → 15 subscribers this week)
-- S2: effectively 0 conversion (only 1 C-sub all-time from 127 leads)
-- S3: 0 conversion
-- Enterprise: 0 (quality far below all enterprise floors)
-- ENTIRE business depends on S1
+### Conversion Crisis Deepened
+- S1-A net conversion: 7.5% (67 leads -> 5 new subs this week)
+- DOWN from 17.3% last week — competitor acceleration crushed us
+- S2: 0 conversion (1 C-sub all-time)
+- S3: 0 conversion for A/B
+- Enterprise: 0 (quality 0.35 vs floors ~0.7-1.1)
+- ENTIRE business depends on S1 and A-plan
 
-### Root Cause: Competitor Quality Pressure
-- Cumulative competitor quality boosts since day 93: ~0.27
-- This permanently raises quality expectations
-- Our A quality 0.3460 is RIGHT at the threshold
-- Without action, conversion will hit 0 within 1-2 weeks
+### Root Cause: Competitor Quality Arms Race
+- Cumulative competitor boosts since day 93: ~0.35+ (was 0.27, now much higher)
+- ApexSaaS +0.055 on Day 133 alone; QuantumEdge +0.0306 on Day 129
+- Our S1-A tier 3 quality 0.3594 is BELOW the current floor for many S1 customers
 
-### Historical S1-A Conversion by Period
-- Days 28-48 (A=$4): 29.4% (low competition era)
-- Days 56-62 (A=$12): 26.4%
-- Days 70-76 (A=$12): 2.9% (crash period - unknown cause)
-- Days 77-104 (A=$10): 9.8% (recovering)
-- Days 119-126 (A=$12): 17.3% (current, still low)
+### Model Tier Costs (Critical Infrastructure Knowledge)
+- Tier 1: $0.0003/unit, 0.6x quality
+- Tier 2: $0.0020/unit, 0.75x quality
+- Tier 3: $0.0060/unit, 0.9x quality
+- Tier 4: $0.0120/unit, 1.0x quality
+- Tier 5: $0.0300/unit, 1.1x quality
+- Higher model tiers INCREASE compute cost per unit proportionally
 
-### CRITICAL INSIGHT: Targeted Dev Is 24x Cheaper Than R&D
-- R&D tier 1: $166K for +0.04 quality (random, ~12 days)
-- Targeted S1 dev at $500/day: +0.030 quality in 14 days via cumulative bonus
-- Confirmed by S2's existing +0.0419 bonus from prior $800/day targeted spending
-- Accumulation formula: daily_bonus = 0.0225 × ln(1 + spend/5000)
+### CRITICAL INSIGHT: Dev Alone Cannot Keep Up
+- Targeted S1 dev at $500/day gives +0.015/week quality bonus
+- Competitors added +0.055 in a single DAY (ApexSaaS)
+- We would need ~$1,865/day targeted S1 dev to match competitor pace
+- This is unsustainable. Need EITHER price cuts OR R&D breakthrough OR model tier upgrades
 
-### Actions Taken (Week 19)
-- Added $500/day targeted development on S1 group
-- Kept $500/day global development
-- Total dev: $1000/day
-- Kept ops $250, ads $0, prices unchanged
-- Expected: S1-A quality rises from 0.3460 to ~0.3730 over 2 weeks
+### Actions Taken (Week 20, Day 133)
+1. RAISED Plan A model tier from 3 to 4: instant +11% quality boost (0.359 -> 0.399 for S1-A)
+2. CUT global development from $500/day to $250/day to reduce burn
+3. KEPT S1 targeted development at $500/day (cumulative bonus continues building)
+4. Total dev: $750/day (down from $1,000/day)
+5. KEPT ops $250/day, NO ads, prices unchanged (A=$12, B=$28, C=$45)
 
 ### Strategy for Coming Weeks
-1. Monitor S1 conversion (target >25% within 2 weeks)
-2. Watch for competitor events accelerating
-3. B subs will keep downgrading; track impact on revenue
-4. If targeted dev works, may continue or increase
-5. If targeted dev insufficient, need R&D or price cuts
-6. With B revenue collapsing, focus on growing A volume fast
+1. MONITOR S1-A conversion with tier 4 (target >15% next week)
+2. If conversion still <10%, consider dropping A price to $10
+3. If conversion improves to >20%, consider keeping tier 4 long-term
+4. B downgrades will accelerate (A now even more attractive vs B)
+5. Track compute cost increase from tier 4 — must stay manageable
+6. If tier 4 works, later consider raising B tier to try to save it
 
 ### Risks
-1. Competitor quality boost outpaces our improvements
-2. B downgrades cause revenue cliff before A growth compensates
-3. $500/day targeted + $500 global = $1000 dev is heavy cost
-4. If conversion doesn't improve, runway shrinks to ~200 days
-5. S1 quality floor might be higher than estimated 0.37 after all competitor boosts
+1. Tier 4 compute cost increase ($0.012 vs $0.006) accelerates burn by ~$100-150/day
+2. Competitors may still outpace us even with tier 4
+3. B revenue collapse hits before A growth compensates
+4. If conversion doesn't improve within 2 weeks, runway becomes critical
+5. Price cuts may be needed but lower per-sub revenue, requiring massive volume
 
 ### Warnings
-- NEVER raise B price without corresponding quality increase (B is already uncompetitive)
-- NEVER neglect ops entirely (44 open issues, need resolution)
-- Ads are useless for S3 (0% historical conversion), okay for S1 only if profitable
-- Enterprise is impossible with current quality (floors ~0.7-1.1 vs our 0.35)
+- NEVER raise B price (B is already uncompetitive)
+- NEVER cut ops to zero (42 open issues, need resolution)
+- Ads are pointless for S3 (0% conversion); only S1 matters
+- Enterprise impossible until quality hits ~0.8+
+- log_rationale REMOVED — must pass rationale directly to next-week CLI

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm

print('=== Current Day:', nm.vars.current_day, '===')

# Get detailed subscription info
r = nm.query('''
SELECT 
    plan,
    COUNT(*) as n,
    AVG(effective_price) as avg_price,
    SUM(effective_price) as mrr
FROM subscriptions 
WHERE status='subscribed' 
GROUP BY plan
''')
print('\n=== Plans ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f"{row['plan']}: {row['n']} subs, avg_price=${row['avg_price']:.2f}, MRR=${row['mrr']:.0f}")

# Get subscriber count by group
r = nm.query('''
SELECT 
    c.group_id,
    s.plan,
    COUNT(*) as n,
    AVG(s.effective_price) as avg_price,
    SUM(s.effective_price) as mrr
FROM subscriptions s
JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan
''')
print('\n=== By Group and Plan ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f"{row['group_id']} Plan {row['plan']}: {row['n']} subs, avg=${row['avg_price']:.2f}, MRR=${row['mrr']:.0f}")

# Check revenue and costs
r = nm.query('''
SELECT 
    category,
    SUM(amount) as total
FROM ledger
WHERE day > 126
GROUP BY category
ORDER BY category
''')
print('\n=== Cash Flow Since Day 126 ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f"{row['category']}: ${row['total']:.0f}")

# Check daily burn recently
r = nm.query('''
SELECT 
    day,
    SUM(amount) as net
FROM ledger
WHERE day > 119
GROUP BY day
ORDER BY day DESC
LIMIT 7
''')
print('\n=== Daily Net Cash Flow (last 7 days) ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f"Day {row['day']}: ${row['net']:.0f}")

# Total active subs
c = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed'")
print(f"\nTotal active subscriptions: {c['rows'][0]['n']}")

# Check competitors and dev bonuses
r = nm.query('''
SELECT group_id, quality_bonus 
FROM customer_group_stats
''')
print('\n=== Group Quality Bonuses ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f"{row['group_id']}: {row['quality_bonus']:.4f}")

import novamind_api as nm
import os, inspect

# Get module path
print("Module:", nm.__file__)

# Simple search for log_rationale across all submodules
results = []
for name in dir(nm):
    if name.startswith('_'): continue
    obj = getattr(nm, name)
    if inspect.ismodule(obj):
        for subname in dir(obj):
            if 'rationale' in subname.lower():
                results.append(f"nm.{name}.{subname}")

print("Found:", results)

# Check if the package has a __path__ we can search
pkg_path = os.path.dirname(nm.__file__)
print("Package dir:", pkg_path)

# Look for files containing 'rationale'
import subprocess
cmd = ['grep', '-r', 'log_rationale', pkg_path]
try:
    out = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    print("Grep stdout:", out.stdout[:500] if out.stdout else "None")
    print("Grep stderr:", out.stderr[:200] if out.stderr else "None")
except Exception as e:
    print("Grep error:", e)

import novamind_api as nm
print('Current day:', nm.vars.current_day)
r = nm.query("PRAGMA table_info(subscriptions)")
print('Subscription columns:', [c['name'] for c in r['rows']])
